title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While investigating the cave, Stephen Lane retrieves a deep green iron box labeled "sandwich" from a high rock crevice. Inspector Philip examines the contents, which include small containers for salt, pepper, and mustard. While the salt and pepper containers are filled with their respective contents, a rectangular flat box contains a bitter, white crystalline powder that Philip identifies as a drug.

At the hotel, Colonel Weston and Hercule Poirot discuss the implications of the drug with the police commissioner. Weston suggests that the victim, Mrs. Marshall, may have been killed after stumbling upon a drug cartel, but Poirot doubts she was an addict due to her healthy appearance. Horace Blatt, a local man, joins the discussion and claims he was sailing alone toward Plymouth at 9:45 a.m. Blatt suggests the murder was a crime of passion and describes Mrs. Marshall as a "dark horse" who had been involved in physical altercations. When asked whom Mrs. Marshall might have been visiting at Fairy Bay, Blatt suggests Redfern, Gardner, Barry, or a clergyman, whom he characterizes as a hypocrite. Blatt also expresses dissatisfaction with the atmosphere at the Pirate's Flag Inn, and Poirot observes that Blatt appears tense. Meanwhile, Inspector Cargraves notes that it takes only three minutes to walk from the hotel to the elevator leading to Fairy Bay, suggesting a person could reach the area quickly once out of sight of other guests.
